content: Circuit breakers halt trading temporarily during extreme price moves to prevent panic and allow information dissemination. Market-wide circuit breakers trigger on S&P 500 declines: Level 1 (7% drop) halts 15 minutes; Level 2 (13%) halts 15 minutes; Level 3 (20%) halts remainder of day. Individual stock circuit breakers (Limit Up-Limit Down) pause trading if price moves outside specified bands within 5 minutes. Cooling-off periods aim to prevent disorderly trading, allow margin calls, and let investors process information. Implemented after 1987 crash, refined after 2010 flash crash.
